slides.title: The Union Dilemma 🧩
slides.content:
When a parameter has a Union type like `id: number | string`, TypeScript gets highly protective.

It will NOT let you call a string-specific method (like `.toUpperCase()`) on `id`, because at runtime, `id` might actually be a number, which would crash the code!

slides.title: The Error

quizData.question: What is the only type of operation TypeScript allows directly on a raw Union variable?

options.text: Operations that are valid for ALL types in the union.

quizData.explanation: TypeScript only allows operations that are common (overlapping) between all the types in the union.

slides.title: Narrowing the Type 🔍
slides.content:
We solve this by checking what type it currently is using standard JavaScript `typeof` logic.

Once inside the `if` block, TypeScript magically "narrows" the type and knows it's 100% safe.

slides.title: Type Guards
slides.content: This `if` statement is called a Type Guard.
